relationships.description: XAI-DataOps Core Principles implement the NIST AI RMF Explainable and Interpretable trustworthy AI characteristic with concrete, actionable principles from NISTIR 8312.
XAI Core Principles support NIST AI RMF Accountability and Transparency by providing the mechanisms through which AI system decisions can be made transparent and accountable.
The Meaningful Explanations principle implements EU AI Act Article 13's requirement that high-risk AI systems be sufficiently transparent for deployers to interpret outputs.
Explanation Accuracy principle implements NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.5 requirement to test whether explanations faithfully represent the system's actual process.
Knowledge Limits principle relates to NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.8 transparency assessment, ensuring systems communicate operational boundaries.
AI System Disclosure practice implements EU AI Act Article 50 transparency obligations for informing individuals of AI interaction and AI-generated content.
Instructions for Use practice directly implements EU AI Act Article 13(3) requirement for clear documentation accompanying high-risk AI systems.
Audit Trails and Decision Logging implements ISO 42001 A.8.1 Transparency of AI systems control by providing systematic evidence of AI system operations and decisions.
Algorithmic Impact Assessment implements ISO 42005 AI System Impact Assessment requirements with specific focus on explainability adequacy for affected stakeholders.
Bias Detection and Fairness Auditing supports NIST AI RMF Fair with Harmful Bias Managed by using XAI techniques to detect and document discriminatory patterns.
Model Cards implement NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.8 transparency assessment through standardized model documentation covering capabilities, limitations, and ethical considerations.
Monitoring and Observability supports NIST AI RMF MANAGE 3 by continuously tracking explanation quality, model drift, and operational boundaries in production.
Stakeholder Explanations framework implements ISO 42001 B.2.3 Explainability and Interpretability objective by defining audience-specific explanation strategies.
Affected Person Explanations implement EU AI Act rights of affected persons, ensuring individuals subject to AI decisions can understand and challenge outcomes.
Counterfactual Explanations support EU AI Act affected person rights by providing actionable recourse information showing what would need to change for a different outcome.
Regulator and Auditor Explanations support EU AI Act conformity assessment by providing compliance-oriented documentation and evidence of systematic explainability.
Data Lineage and Provenance supports ISO 42001 A.6.2 data management controls by providing comprehensive traceability from data origin through model output.
Regulatory Alignment supports NIST AI RMF GOVERN 1 by mapping organizational XAI practices to applicable regulatory requirements and voluntary frameworks.
